message:
🌱 Add kube-vip: svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same mission (#951)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kube-vip-879-svc-election-true-will-cause-load-balancers-with-the-same-ip-to-app",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kube-vip: svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same IP to appear on different servers",
+    "description": "svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same IP to appear on different servers. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kube-vip deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kube-vip version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
helm list -n kube-vip 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kube-vip installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kube-vip configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kube-vip configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
kubectl get configmap -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-vip
```
I have a three-node Kubernetes cluster with two load balancers using the same IP but different port numbers. This VIP appears on two nodes simultaneously.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same…",
+        "description": "This should fix #879

This PR introduces new annotation `kube-vip.io/leaseName: <name>` that will tie multiple services with single lease (so only one node will be a leader)..

Additionally fixed some issues I found with IP address deletion in ARP mode.

Edit:

Added support for leader election in
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
data:
  cidr-qt-system: 10.106.109.9/32
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    meta.helm.sh/release-name: kube-vip-cloud-provider
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
  name: kubevip
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
kubectl get events -n kube-vip --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "svc_election=true will cause load balancers with the same…" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This should fix #879

This PR introduces new annotation `kube-vip.io/leaseName: <name>` that will tie multiple services with single lease (so only one node will be a leader)..

Additionally fixed some issues I found with IP address deletion in ARP mode.

Edit:

Added support for leader election in BGP mode and e2e tests for RT and BGP leader election with common lease.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: v1
data:
  cidr-qt-system: 10.106.109.9/32
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    meta.helm.sh/release-name: kube-vip-cloud-provider
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
  name: kubevip",
+        "apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  annotations:
    kube-vip.io/loadbalancerIPs: 10.106.109.9
  labels:
    implementation: kube-vip
  name: traefik-lb-1
spec:
  ports:
  - name: agent
    port: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: agent
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: traefik
    app.kubernetes.io/name: traefik
  type: LoadBalancer",
+        "apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  annotations:
    kube-vip.io/loadbalancerIPs: 10.106.109.9
  labels:
    implementation: kube-vip
  name: traefik-lb-2
spec:
  ports:
  - name: web
    port: 80
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: web
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: traefik
    app.kubernetes.io/name: traefik
  type: LoadBalancer"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
